workLog.action: Authored REQ-008, DESIGN-008, TASK-008 spec artifacts and INTERVIEW-orphan-ref-validator capturing PR1 wedge scope: AC1, AC2, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC9 subset
Implemented .claude/skills/orphan-ref-validator/ with scripts/scan.py and tests/test_scan.py; 34 tests pass

Self-test on source repo (TASK-008-07) surfaced false positives in ADRs and docs/. Iteratively refined: regex tightening (require trailing alphanumeric), denylist expansion (model IDs, frontmatter fields, eval verdicts, git hooks, PowerShell/npm modules), opt-in flags --include-adrs and --include-skill-descriptions, exclude-dir-names for references/templates/__pycache__, file-scope <!-- orphan-ref-ignore-file --> directive, line-scope <!-- orphan-ref-ignore --> directive, glob pattern support in target paths

Default scope decision: specs + evals + manifests by default; ADRs and skill descriptions opt-in. Documented in DESIGN-008 with rationale (ADRs reference proposed/deleted entities by name; skill descriptions have widespread preexisting drift)

Applied file-scope ignore directives to historical specs (REQ-007, TASK-007, DESIGN-007 for M1 deletion; REQ-003 for plugin toolkit names; mcp-integration-overview, agent-orchestration-mcp-spec, session-state-mcp-spec, skill-catalog-mcp-spec for proposed memory entities)
Applied line-scope ignore directives to specific lines in REQ-002, REQ-007, REQ-008, TASK-008, INTERVIEW-1884, DESIGN-008 where individual references are intentional
Fixed real orphan: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json count claim from 23 agents/23 commands/67 skills to actual 25 agents/13 commands/68 skills
Wired orphan-ref-validator as fourth Mandatory Exit Gate in .claude/commands/build.md with VERDICT: CRITICAL_FAIL blocking semantics
Final self-test: 70 files scanned, 71 refs checked, 0 findings, VERDICT: PASS, exit code 0
Refactored denylist into filters.py module per taste-lints file-size cap; renamed from _filters.py to satisfy snake_case naming rule. scan.py 485 lines under cap. 34 tests pass, scan PASS

Applied file-scope ignore to MCP integration spec, agent-orchestration-mcp-spec, session-state-mcp-spec, skill-catalog-mcp-spec; line-scope ignore to REQ-002 and INTERVIEW-1884; updated REQ-008 internal AC paths to use absolute skill-relative form

Real fix: marketplace.json count claim updated from 23 agents/23 commands/67 skills to actual 25 agents/13 commands/68 skills. Wired orphan-ref-validator as fourth Mandatory Exit Gate in .claude/commands/build.md.

Pre-push surfaced two blockers: marketplace count divergence with canonical validator_marketplace_counts.py (my naive iterdir counted templates/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md), and security-suppression comment. Aligned enumerate_count to canonical strategies per canonical-source-mirror.md (excluded AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md from agents; recursive walk for commands). Replaced try/except ImportError with __package__ check to remove type: ignore. Ran build/scripts/build_all.py to regenerate src/copilot-cli/skills/orphan-ref-validator/ and src/copilot-cli/skills/build/SKILL.md mirrors.

PR review feedback round 1: 19 inline comments. Critical fixes - (1) Success envelope contract per ADR-056: Success now reflects scan execution not finding presence, true on CRITICAL_FAIL with verdict expressing it; (2) _resolve_repo_root raises RepoRootError on missing/non-dir paths, main maps to exit 2; (3) enumerate_skills returns None when dir absent (was returning empty set, masked downstream warn-vs-critical disambiguation); (4) COUNT_CLAIM_RE rewritten to mirror canonical COUNT_PATTERN byte-for-byte (handles 'reusable skills', 'slash commands', 'lifecycle hooks', 'specialized agent definitions'); (5) enumerate_count switches on canonical labels with legacy aliases; (6) _walk_targets uses iterdir+prune instead of rglob+postfilter for performance; (7) walked-file containment recheck post symlink resolution (CWE-22 hardening); (8) count_claim emission delegated to canonical validate_marketplace_counts.py per canonical-source-mirror.md (multi-plugin manifests need YAML config); enforce_counts param reserved for PR2; (9) TASK-007 frontmatter merged into one block (assignee was outside metadata); (10) DESIGN-008 added required sections (Requirements Addressed, Design Overview, Component Details, Technology Decisions, Security Considerations, Testing Strategy); (11) REQ-008/DESIGN-008/TASK-008 deferred-list updated to reflect AC3 ships in PR1 and AC4 emission delegated; (12) SKILL.md flag table reflects actual defaults (no .claude/skills/ in default scope); (13) build.md gate 4 uses Skill() invocation instead of hardcoded path. 40 tests pass; self-test PASS.

PR review feedback round 2: per-call enumerate_count cache to eliminate redundant filesystem walks per gemini perf comment; REQ-008 and DESIGN-008 related fields complete forward-trace (REQ -> DESIGN -> TASK); TASK-008 status changed from draft to in-progress and complexity: M added to satisfy spec validator; episode-2026-05-10-session-1830.json hand-rewritten with valid event-type enums (milestone/error/recovery), accurate metrics (9 commits, 32 files, 240 min, 2 errors, 2 recoveries), explicit decisions, and three lessons learned; test_scan.py uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location with file-keyed module names to avoid sys.modules collision between .claude/skills and src/copilot-cli/skills mirrored test suites.

PR review feedback round 3: 13 new comments. Critical fixes - (1) REQ-008 had two YAML 'related' keys (the second silently dropped DESIGN/TASK refs); merged into one block with REQ-007, DESIGN-008, TASK-008. (2) scan() coerced None from enumerate_skills with 'or set()' which defeated the warn-vs-critical disambiguation; now passes skill_catalog_present flag through scan_file so absent .claude/skills/ produces warn (not critical) findings while empty .claude/skills/ stays critical. Two new tests cover both cases; test count 40 -> 42. (3) --repo-root help text rewritten to describe the actual walk-up-to-.git resolution and the ADR-035 exit-2 behavior on bad paths. (4) __init__.py docstring rewritten to match the importlib.util loader pattern. (5) SKILL.md and build.md gate description rewritten to truthfully say count_claim emission is delegated to canonical validate_marketplace_counts.py (was previously claiming the gate catches count drift). (6) DESIGN-008 test-case count updated to 42.

Round 4 (5-axis /review verdicts: REQUEST_CHANGES from architect/security/qa + taste-lints FAIL on scan.py 719 lines). Critical fixes: (1) Extract walking.py (CWE-22/CWE-59 hardening: is_safe_subdirectory rejects symlink directories outside repo before recursion); align EXCLUDE_DIR_NAMES with canonical _EXCLUDED_DIRS (added worktrees, cache; kept references, templates as documented divergence). (2) Extract counts.py to mirror canonical-source verbatim and centralize the working-tree enumeration. (3) Extract envelope.py with Finding/ScanResult and render_envelope/render_error_envelope; expose render_error_envelope for the exit-2 path so configuration errors emit ADR-056 Success=false envelope (was unreachable). (4) Extend secret denylist (id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa, id_dsa, .pfx, .p12, .netrc, .npmrc, .pypirc, credentials). (5) enumerate_skills returns None when .claude/skills/ is a regular file (vendored-install corruption); was raising NotADirectoryError. (6) Decompose _iter_dir_pruned into _iter_entry + _within_size_cap to satisfy cyclomatic complexity ≤10. (7) Document ignore-directive bypass as intentional with code-review compensating control in DESIGN-008. New tests for symlink-to-directory containment, .claude/skills as a file, CWD-not-in-git fallback, glob target expansion, and exit-2 envelope shape (47 cases). scan.py 719 -> 455 lines; all 5 modules clean under taste-lints (0 errors).

Round 5 (12 review threads + 2 CI fails after round-4 push). Critical fixes: (1) STEP-0-METRICS.md was accidentally committed to .agents/sessions/ in round-4 git add -A; the path triggered Session Protocol Validation which expected JSON via Convert-SessionToJson.ps1 (script absent). Moved file to .agents/metrics/STEP-0-METRICS.md to remove from session validator scope. (2) --include-adrs and --include-skill-descriptions silently ignored when --targets supplied; now log a WARNING explaining the precedence. (3) reset_count_cache() now called at scan() entry; programmatic re-scans no longer see stale counts (devin's observation about latent PR2 bug). Doc accuracy fixes: SKILL.md verification checklist test count 34 -> 47 (and now reads 'currently 47 cases; count grows'); EXCLUDE_DIR_NAMES list now lists worktrees/cache; Reference detection table corrected (skill_name regex requires hyphen; count_claim shows canonical COUNT_PATTERN with delegation note); Path safety section lists the full secret denylist; Failure modes table replaced count_claim WARN row with delegation explanation and split symlink-loop row into directory/file. TASK-008 milestone count 42 -> 47. Serena memory aligned with shipped state (AC3 implemented; canonical 23/23/35/68 counts).

Round 6 (/test 6-gate run: NFR analyst WARN with 1 critical + DX critic NEEDS_REVISION with 5 critical). NFR fixes: (1) scan_file refactored from 127-line method into a thin orchestrator delegating to _check_skill_refs / _check_script_refs / _check_count_claims plus _count_warn_finding / _count_critical_finding helpers. (2) _WHITESPACE_RE compiled module-scope (was inline re.sub per match). (3) counts.py walks now apply _EXCLUDED_DIR_NAMES future-proofing (defense in depth if a vendor dir ever lands in .claude). (4) MAX_FINDINGS=500 cap with synthetic warn finding bounds memory on pathological repos. (5) Patterns extracted to patterns.py (regex + extractors + line ignore directive helper); scan.py 560 -> 478 lines. DX fixes: (1) --include-* warning emitted to stderr regardless of log level. (2) build.md gate 4 description includes the diagnostic invocation literal. (3) SKILL.md adds dedicated 'Ignore directives' subsection with placement rules and the 50-line window. (4) New 'When the /build gate fails' section with three-step recovery + 'Investigation workflow' for opt-in flags. (5) Fixed pytest invocation inconsistency (uv run pytest everywhere). 48 tests pass; scan PASS; all 6 modules clean under taste-lints (0 errors).

Round 7 (24 review threads). Critical fixes: (1) Error.Code now integer 2 + Type 'InvalidParams' per skill-output.schema.json (was string 'ConfigurationError', schema violation). (2) File-symlink containment: walking.py _is_safe_file resolves and rejects file symlinks whose target escapes repo_root (was: only directories checked). Test added. (3) In-repo symlink cycle guard: _iter_dir_pruned tracks visited canonical paths via Path.resolve() and short-circuits on revisit. Test added. (4) walk_targets applies SCAN_FILE_SUFFIXES to direct file targets (was: only enforced during directory walk). Test added. (5) REQ-008 category 'maintainability' -> 'non-functional' (valid spec enum). (6) VERDICT: ERROR documented in REQ-008 AC5, SKILL.md, Serena memory (was: contract said only PASS|WARN|CRITICAL_FAIL but ERROR shipped). (7) build.md gate 4 documents repo-wide scope rationale and points at directives for opt-out. (8) Mirror-divergence fixes (Extension Points pointing at scan.py for *_REF_RE -> patterns.py; build.md diagnostic command no longer hardcodes path). (9) Canonical-mirror docstrings rewritten: walking.py now quotes _EXCLUDED_DIRS byte-for-byte, counts.py quotes the project-toolkit YAML stanza byte-for-byte, both list divergences honestly. (10) Test count drift: removed pinned numbers from SKILL.md / TASK-008 / DESIGN-008 / Serena memory. 51 tests pass; scan PASS; 6 modules clean.
